> **DESIGN PREMISE:**
> Intelligence is not a single number. It is a multi-dimensional cognitive
> profile — spanning linguistic, logical-mathematical, spatial, kinesthetic,
> musical, interpersonal, intrapersonal, naturalistic, creative, and debate
> dimensions — that evolves across a lifetime from age 5 to age 60.
> The Intelligence Report Generator Agent (IRGA) is the synthesis layer
> of the entire Ecoskiller Antigravity platform. It draws from every upstream
> agent, every evaluation signal, every skill vector, and every behavioral
> trace to produce audience-specific, role-gated, time-bound intelligence
> reports. These reports are NOT raw data dumps. They are structured,
> narrative-enriched, evidence-anchored intelligence portraits designed for
> five distinct audiences: Students, Parents, Teachers/Mentors, Recruiters,
> and Institutes — each with a completely different information architecture,
> permission boundary, and communication register.
> IRGA is the most data-integrative agent in the Ecoskiller ecosystem.
> It synthesizes signals from OREA, CDA, DQAA, FEATURE_STORE, BELT_ENGINE,
> RATING_ENGINE, GROWTH_ENGINE, TOOL_SYNC, and CHAMPIONSHIP systems.
> It never generates data. It only synthesizes, structures, and renders
> pre-computed, confidence-validated intelligence signals into governed,
> audience-appropriate reports.

## ▶ SECTION 2 — PURPOSE DECLARATION

### 2.1 The Problem This Agent Solves

The Ecoskiller Antigravity platform produces enormous volumes of evaluation
signals across OREA (open response), CDA (creativity divergence), DQAA (debate
quality), belt progressions, championship records, tool-sync artifacts, and
passive behavioral intelligence vectors. These signals are precise, auditable,
and machine-readable — but they are not human-readable in their raw form.

No parent looking at a child's performance dashboard wants to read JSON feature
vectors. No recruiter wants to parse confidence calibration logs. No institute
wants raw percentile distributions without context, narrative, or actionable
structure.

The **Intelligence Report Generator Agent (IRGA)** solves this by:

1. **Pulling from the full intelligence profile** built across all upstream agents
2. **Mapping signals to nine intelligence dimensions** (Howard Gardner-inspired,
   platform-adapted for professional and educational contexts)
3. **Generating five completely distinct report formats** — each tuned to its
   audience's language, permission level, information need, and action context
4. **Producing growth narratives** — not just scores, but trajectory stories
5. **Generating career intelligence mapping** — connecting intelligence profile
   to career pathway recommendations
6. **Emitting alerts** when intelligence signals cross defined thresholds
7. **Supporting institutional intelligence aggregation** — cohort-level reports
   for institutes and enterprises without exposing individual user data
8. **Powering the intelligence radar chart** — the visual centerpiece of every
   user profile on the platform

### 2.2 What This Agent Does NOT Do

- It does NOT compute raw evaluation scores (all scores come from upstream agents)
- It does NOT conduct intelligence tests (ADAPTIVE_TESTING_ENGINE handles this)
- It does NOT make hiring decisions, belt awards, or certification decisions
- It does NOT access cross-tenant data for any comparison
- It does NOT share Student-level detail with Recruiters without user consent
- It does NOT share individual user data with Institute reports (aggregated only)
- It does NOT generate speculative intelligence assessments without signal evidence
- It does NOT produce reports for users with insufficient signal data below minimum
  threshold (INSUFFICIENT_DATA policy applies)
- It does NOT override privacy settings or consent flags on any user record

### 2.3 Upstream Signal Sources

| Source Agent / System | Intelligence Signals Provided |
|---|---|
| `FEATURE_STORE_AGENT` | Aggregated feature vectors from all evaluation agents |
| `OREA` | Open response competency scores, confidence vectors |
| `CDA` | Five creativity divergence dimension scores, CDI, originality signal |
| `DQAA` | Seven debate quality dimension scores, DQI, argument map metrics |
| `BELT_ENGINE` | Belt records per skill per domain, belt history, promotion trail |
| `RATING_ENGINE` | Match ratings per domain, Elo-equivalent ratings |
| `GROWTH_ENGINE` | XP records, streak data, achievement badge history |
| `SKILL_ENGINE` | Skill level per skill per domain, skill gap analysis signals |
| `ADAPTIVE_TESTING_ENGINE` | Intelligence test scores per dimension, reliability scores |
| `CHAMPIONSHIP_ENGINE` | Championship participation, rank, award records |
| `INTEGRATION_ENGINE` | Tool-sync skill signals (GitHub, Figma, Jira, Salesforce, etc.) |
| `CAREER_AI_AGENT` | Career pathway recommendations, placement probability signals |
| `MENTOR_CONTROL_ENGINE` | Mentor observations (structured, not narrative) |
| `PASSIVE_INTELLIGENCE_PIPELINE` | Behavioral signals: focus, consistency, learning speed |

### 2.4 Downstream Recipients

| Recipient / Agent | Data Delivered |
|---|---|
| `NOTIFICATION_SERVICE` | Report ready alerts per audience |
| `STUDENT_DASHBOARD` | Full student self-view intelligence report |
| `PARENT_DASHBOARD` | Parent-scoped child intelligence report |
| `TEACHER_MENTOR_DASHBOARD` | Educator-scoped student intelligence report |
| `RECRUITER_SYSTEM` | Recruiter-scoped candidate intelligence report |
| `INSTITUTE_ANALYTICS_ENGINE` | Aggregated cohort intelligence reports |
| `GOVERNANCE_AGENT` | Audit records for every report generation event |
| `OBSERVABILITY_AGENT` | Performance metrics, error rates, generation latency |
| `CAREER_AI_AGENT` | Refreshed intelligence profile → career path update trigger |
| `GROWTH_ENGINE` | Report generation event → milestone badge check |

## ▶ SECTION 3 — NINE INTELLIGENCE DIMENSIONS (LOCKED)

*These dimensions are locked at v1.0.0. Addition requires MAJOR version bump.*
*No dimension may be removed without full governance cycle and lifetime data impact audit.*
*Dimension names map to the Intelligence Radar Chart in the Student profile.*

---

### Dimension 1 — LINGUISTIC INTELLIGENCE (LIN)

```yaml
DEFINITION:
  Capacity to use language with precision, nuance, and effectiveness —
  both in production (written/spoken output) and comprehension.
  Encompasses vocabulary range, syntactic complexity, argumentative
  structure, and communicative adaptability.

PRIMARY_SIGNAL_SOURCES:
  - OREA: response_coherence, lexical_diversity_score, argument_structure_score
  - DQAA: ARG dimension score, PER dimension score (persuasion mechanics)
  - CDA: FLU dimension (idea fluency in language), ELA dimension (elaboration depth)
  - ADAPTIVE_TESTING_ENGINE: linguistic intelligence test score (where taken)
  - INTEGRATION_ENGINE: Notion/Google Docs writing quality signals

COMPOSITE_COMPUTATION:
  WEIGHTED_AVERAGE of normalized signals from above sources
  Weights per source: defined in SIGNAL_WEIGHT_TABLE (Section 6.2)
  Minimum signals required: 3 distinct source signals
  Below minimum: INSUFFICIENT_DATA flag for this dimension

GROWTH_TRACKED:         YES — LIN timeline built from all historical evaluations
CAREER_MAPPING:         High LIN → content creation, legal, journalism, education,
                        sales, marketing, public affairs, advocacy roles
RADAR_AXIS:             "Linguistic" on intelligence radar chart
SCORING_RANGE:          0.0 – 100.0 (normalized to domain cohort)
```

---

### Dimension 2 — LOGICAL-MATHEMATICAL INTELLIGENCE (LOG)

```yaml
DEFINITION:
  Capacity for logical reasoning, pattern recognition, numerical
  processing, systematic problem-solving, and causal analysis.
  The foundation of analytical and scientific thinking.

PRIMARY_SIGNAL_SOURCES:
  - OREA: argument_structure_score, logical_coherence_proxy (where available)
  - DQAA: LOG dimension (logical coherence), REB dimension (rebuttal logic precision)
  - ADAPTIVE_TESTING_ENGINE: logical-mathematical intelligence test score
  - SKILL_ENGINE: mathematics, data analysis, coding skill levels
  - INTEGRATION_ENGINE: GitHub code complexity signals, data tool signals
  - CHAMPIONSHIP_ENGINE: performance in logic/reasoning championship events

COMPOSITE_COMPUTATION:
  WEIGHTED_AVERAGE of normalized signals
  Minimum signals required: 3 distinct source signals

GROWTH_TRACKED:         YES
CAREER_MAPPING:         High LOG → engineering, data science, finance, research,
                        law, medicine, architecture, strategic consulting
RADAR_AXIS:             "Logical" on intelligence radar chart
SCORING_RANGE:          0.0 – 100.0
```

---

### Dimension 3 — SPATIAL INTELLIGENCE (SPA)

```yaml
DEFINITION:
  Capacity to think in visual and spatial terms — to mentally
  manipulate, transform, and reason about objects, layouts,
  patterns, and environments. Relevant to design, architecture,
  engineering visualization, and creative visual work.

PRIMARY_SIGNAL_SOURCES:
  - ADAPTIVE_TESTING_ENGINE: spatial intelligence test score
  - INTEGRATION_ENGINE: Figma design complexity signals, Canva output signals
  - SKILL_ENGINE: design skill levels (Figma, AutoCAD, etc.)
  - CHAMPIONSHIP_ENGINE: design/spatial championships
  - OREA: spatial reasoning question performance (where scenario applies)

COMPOSITE_COMPUTATION:
  WEIGHTED_AVERAGE of normalized signals
  Minimum signals required: 2 distinct source signals
  Note: SPA has fewer signal sources than other dimensions —
        confidence often lower; flagged when below 0.60

GROWTH_TRACKED:         YES
CAREER_MAPPING:         High SPA → UI/UX design, architecture, engineering,
                        urban planning, surgery, fine arts, game design
RADAR_AXIS:             "Spatial" on intelligence radar chart
SCORING_RANGE:          0.0 – 100.0
```

---

### Dimension 4 — INTERPERSONAL INTELLIGENCE (ITP)

```yaml
DEFINITION:
  Capacity to understand, interpret, and effectively navigate
  social dynamics, group behavior, leadership contexts, and
  collaborative environments. Measured through group performance
  signals, not self-report.

PRIMARY_SIGNAL_SOURCES:
  - DQAA: ENG dimension (session engagement, participation equity),
           ADA dimension (adaptability under social pressure),
           cross_speaker_reference signals
  - OREA: Group discussion rubric performance
  - PASSIVE_INTELLIGENCE_PIPELINE: collaboration behavioral signals,
           response_to_challenge patterns, peer interaction consistency
  - MENTOR_CONTROL_ENGINE: structured mentor observations on collaborative behavior
  - CHAMPIONSHIP_ENGINE: team competition performance records
  - RATING_ENGINE: rating trajectory in team-format events

COMPOSITE_COMPUTATION:
  WEIGHTED_AVERAGE of normalized signals
  Minimum signals required: 3 distinct source signals

GROWTH_TRACKED:         YES
CAREER_MAPPING:         High ITP → leadership, HR, sales management, diplomacy,
                        counseling, team management, entrepreneurship,
                        community development, education
RADAR_AXIS:             "Interpersonal" on intelligence radar chart
SCORING_RANGE:          0.0 – 100.0
```

### Dimension 5 — INTRAPERSONAL INTELLIGENCE (INP)

```yaml
DEFINITION:
  Capacity for accurate self-knowledge, self-regulation, goal
  persistence, and reflective learning. Measured through behavioral
  consistency, growth response patterns, and goal achievement signals.

PRIMARY_SIGNAL_SOURCES:
  - PASSIVE_INTELLIGENCE_PIPELINE: consistency score, streak persistence,
           login regularity, goal completion rate, recovery_after_failure patterns
  - GROWTH_ENGINE: streak history, milestone achievement rate, comeback signals
  - ADAPTIVE_TESTING_ENGINE: intrapersonal intelligence test score
  - DQAA: ADA dimension (self-regulation under pressure)
  - SKILL_ENGINE: self-directed skill completion rate, roadmap adherence
  - BELT_ENGINE: deliberate practice signal (repeated attempts on same skill)

COMPOSITE_COMPUTATION:
  WEIGHTED_AVERAGE of normalized behavioral signals
  Minimum signals required: 3 distinct source signals

GROWTH_TRACKED:         YES
CAREER_MAPPING:         High INP → entrepreneurship, research, writing,
                        philosophy, therapy, coaching, independent consulting,
                        spiritual and meaning-centered vocations
RADAR_AXIS:             "Intrapersonal" on intelligence radar chart
SCORING_RANGE:          0.0 – 100.0
```

### Dimension 6 — CREATIVE INTELLIGENCE (CRE)

```yaml
DEFINITION:
  Capacity for divergent thinking, original idea generation,
  combinatorial novelty, and flexible cognitive category-crossing.
  Directly sourced from the Creativity Divergence Agent (CDA).

PRIMARY_SIGNAL_SOURCES:
  - CDA: composite_cdi (Creativity Divergence Index), all five CDA dimensions:
           FLU (fluency), FLX (flexibility), ORI (originality),
           ELA (elaboration), SRX (surprise index)
  - CHAMPIONSHIP_ENGINE: creativity-specific championship performance
  - INTEGRATION_ENGINE: tool-sync creative artifact signals (Figma, Canva, Notion)
  - ADAPTIVE_TESTING_ENGINE: creative intelligence test score (where taken)
  - IDEA_DNA_AGENT: originality_threshold_met events count

COMPOSITE_COMPUTATION:
  CDA composite_cdi is primary signal (weight: 0.65)
  Other sources contribute remaining 0.35
  Minimum signals required: CDA composite_cdi must be present
  (CRE cannot be computed without at least one CDA evaluation)

GROWTH_TRACKED:         YES — CDI growth_delta feeds CRE trajectory
CAREER_MAPPING:         High CRE → product design, innovation management,
                        creative direction, R&D, entrepreneurship, arts,
                        marketing strategy, UX research, game development
RADAR_AXIS:             "Creative" on intelligence radar chart
SCORING_RANGE:          0.0 – 100.0
```

---

### Dimension 7 — DEBATE INTELLIGENCE (DEB)

```yaml
DEFINITION:
  Capacity for structured argumentation, logical adversarial engagement,
  evidence-based reasoning, and real-time rebuttal under opposition.
  Directly sourced from the Debate Quality Analyzer Agent (DQAA).

PRIMARY_SIGNAL_SOURCES:
  - DQAA: composite_dqi (Debate Quality Index), all seven DQAA dimensions:
           ARG (argument structure), LOG (logical coherence),
           REB (rebuttal precision), EVD (evidence deployment),
           ADA (adversarial adaptability), PER (persuasion mechanics),
           ENG (session engagement)
  - CHAMPIONSHIP_ENGINE: championship debate performance records
  - BELT_ENGINE: debate-track belt progression
  - RATING_ENGINE: debate match rating

COMPOSITE_COMPUTATION:
  DQAA composite_dqi is primary signal (weight: 0.70)
  Other sources contribute remaining 0.30
  Minimum signals required: DQAA composite_dqi must be present
  (DEB cannot be computed without at least one DQAA evaluation)

GROWTH_TRACKED:         YES — DQI growth_delta feeds DEB trajectory
CAREER_MAPPING:         High DEB → law, policy, consulting, executive leadership,
                        sales, negotiation, media, academia, public affairs,
                        journalism, diplomacy
RADAR_AXIS:             "Debate" on intelligence radar chart
SCORING_RANGE:          0.0 – 100.0
```

---

### Dimension 8 — LEARNING VELOCITY (LRV)

```yaml
DEFINITION:
  The speed and efficiency with which a user acquires new skills,
  consolidates learning, and transfers knowledge across domains.
  Not a measure of raw ability — a measure of learning efficiency
  over time relative to domain baseline and cohort.

PRIMARY_SIGNAL_SOURCES:
  - PASSIVE_INTELLIGENCE_PIPELINE: learning_speed_index (time-to-competency per skill),
           retention_check performance, skill_decay_rate
  - BELT_ENGINE: time-to-belt per skill (vs. cohort baseline)
  - SKILL_ENGINE: skill progression velocity (XP per unit time per skill)
  - ADAPTIVE_TESTING_ENGINE: learning velocity test scores
  - OREA: improvement_velocity (delta from prior_attempt)
  - CHAMPIONSHIP_ENGINE: performance trajectory across multiple championship rounds

COMPOSITE_COMPUTATION:
  WEIGHTED_AVERAGE of velocity signals (time-normalized, cohort-relative)
  Minimum signals required: 3 distinct source signals
  Note: LRV requires minimum 30-day data window for meaningful computation

GROWTH_TRACKED:         YES — LRV itself is a trajectory signal; tracked monthly
CAREER_MAPPING:         High LRV → fast-growth startups, consulting, R&D,
                        military/strategy, competitive professional environments,
                        any role requiring rapid domain adaptation
RADAR_AXIS:             "Learning Velocity" on intelligence radar chart
SCORING_RANGE:          0.0 – 100.0 (cohort-normalized percentile velocity)
```

---

### Dimension 9 — FOCUS INTELLIGENCE (FOC)

```yaml
DEFINITION:
  The capacity for sustained attention, deliberate practice persistence,
  task completion under complexity, and resistance to distraction-driven
  performance degradation. Measured through behavioral consistency signals.

PRIMARY_SIGNAL_SOURCES:
  - PASSIVE_INTELLIGENCE_PIPELINE: focus_measurement_index, session_completion_rate,
           task_abandonment_rate, time_on_task consistency
  - SKILL_ENGINE: exercise completion rate, drill engagement rate
  - BELT_ENGINE: deliberate practice attempt frequency (persistence signal)
  - GROWTH_ENGINE: streak length, streak recovery rate
  - ADAPTIVE_TESTING_ENGINE: focus/attention intelligence test score
  - CHAMPIONSHIP_ENGINE: performance under time pressure (pressure context vs. standard)

COMPOSITE_COMPUTATION:
  WEIGHTED_AVERAGE of behavioral signals (time-normalized)
  Minimum signals required: 3 distinct source signals
  Note: FOC requires minimum 14-day behavioral window for meaningful computation

GROWTH_TRACKED:         YES
CAREER_MAPPING:         High FOC → research, surgery, programming, auditing,
                        writing, trading, engineering, watchmaking, any
                        domain requiring sustained deep work
RADAR_AXIS:             "Focus" on intelligence radar chart
SCORING_RANGE:          0.0 – 100.0
```

## ▶ SECTION 4 — FIVE REPORT TYPES (LOCKED)

*Report architecture locked at v1.0.0.*
*Each report type has a distinct information schema, permission boundary,*
*communication register, and data exposure policy.*

---

### Report Type 1 — STUDENT INTELLIGENCE REPORT (SIR)

```yaml
AUDIENCE:               The user themselves (student, professional, any age)
PERMISSION_LEVEL:       FULL SELF-ACCESS — all nine dimensions, all evidence,
                        all growth data, all career recommendations
AGE_ADAPTATION:         YES — communication register, complexity, and visual
                        density adapted for: CHILD (5–12), TEEN (13–17),
                        YOUNG_ADULT (18–25), ADULT (26–45), SENIOR (46–60)
PRIVACY_GATE:           None for own data (subject to user's own consent settings)

MANDATORY_SECTIONS:
  1. Intelligence Radar Chart (all 9 dimensions visualized)
  2. Intelligence Score Summary (composite + per-dimension scores + percentiles)
  3. Strength Profile (top 3 dimensions with evidence narrative)
  4. Growth Zone (bottom 3 dimensions with actionable improvement paths)
  5. Intelligence Timeline (historical trajectory per dimension, min 3 data points)
  6. Intelligence Prediction (30/90/180-day trajectory forecast)
  7. Career Intelligence Map (recommended pathways based on profile)
  8. Skill Intelligence Bridge (how current skills connect to intelligence dimensions)
  9. Championship & Competition Intelligence (performance in competitive contexts)
  10. Achievement Intelligence (belt, badge, XP aligned to intelligence signals)
  11. Personalized Intelligence Goals (3 goals — SMART-structured)
  12. Data Sources & Confidence (transparency block — which agents contributed)

AGE_REGISTER_RULES:
  CHILD (5–12):
    - Simple language (max 6th grade readability)
    - Visual-first (icon + chart > text)
    - Strength-positive framing (no deficit language)
    - Max 5 sections rendered
    - Parental consent required for full report
  TEEN (13–17):
    - Clear, direct language
    - Growth-framing (challenges as opportunities)
    - Career exploration (not prescription)
    - All 12 sections available
  YOUNG_ADULT (18–25):
    - Professional tone
    - Career intelligence prominent
    - Competitive context highlighted
    - All 12 sections available
  ADULT (26–45):
    - Executive register
    - Professional development framing
    - Skill-to-role mapping
    - All 12 sections available
  SENIOR (46–60):
    - Mastery and wisdom framing
    - Legacy and mentorship career paths surfaced
    - Learning velocity contextualized to experience
    - All 12 sections available

GENERATION_TRIGGER:
  - User-requested (on-demand)
  - Monthly scheduled auto-generation
  - Significant growth event trigger (breakthrough, new belt, championship result)
  - Onboarding completion trigger
  - Adaptive test completion trigger
```

---

### Report Type 2 — PARENT INTELLIGENCE REPORT (PIR)

```yaml
AUDIENCE:               Parent or legal guardian of a linked child account
PERMISSION_LEVEL:       PARENT-SCOPED — curated view of child intelligence profile
                        Excludes: peer comparison by name, competitive rankings with
                        identifiable opponents, raw fallacy reports, career financial
                        projections (not age-appropriate for young children)
CHILD_CONSENT:          Teen users (13–17) may control certain parent visibility
                        toggles per privacy settings
PRIVACY_GATE:           Parental link must be verified in TRUST_SYSTEM before access
                        Parent cannot access report for unlinked or rejected links

MANDATORY_SECTIONS:
  1. Intelligence Overview (radar chart — simplified 5-axis view for young children)
  2. Strength Highlights (top 3 strengths in plain, encouraging language)
  3. Growth Opportunities (growth zones — framed positively, not as deficits)
  4. Learning Progress (timeline — monthly view, plain language)
  5. Activity Summary (how much learning activity, engagement level, consistency)
  6. Championship & Achievement Highlights (competitions participated, belts earned)
  7. Recommended Next Steps (3 parent-actionable recommendations)
  8. School/Institute Coordination Signal (if institute is linked — brief summary)
  9. Wellbeing Indicators (focus, consistency, engagement health signals)
  10. Intelligence Goals (child's current goals, progress toward them)

LANGUAGE_REGISTER:
  - Warm, supportive, non-clinical tone
  - Strengths-first framing throughout
  - No raw scores exposed without context
  - No deficit language ("weak at," "fails at," "below average")
  - Progress language ("developing," "growing," "emerging strength")
  - Age-specific framing for child's age band

FORBIDDEN_IN_PARENT_REPORT:
  - Individual competitor identities
  - Raw confidence scores without explanation
  - Technical ML/AI model details
  - Financial projections
  - Speculative career prescriptions (child career exploration OK, prescription NOT)
  - Any data the child has explicitly marked private from parents (privacy toggle)

GENERATION_TRIGGER:
  - Parent-requested (on-demand, max 4 per month)
  - Monthly scheduled auto-generation
  - Significant achievement event (new belt, championship win, breakthrough)
  - Parental alert trigger (from INTELLIGENCE_ALERT_SYSTEM)
```

---

### Report Type 3 — TEACHER / MENTOR INTELLIGENCE REPORT (TMIR)

```yaml
AUDIENCE:               Certified teachers, mentors, trainers, and evaluators
                        assigned to the student in the platform
PERMISSION_LEVEL:       EDUCATOR-SCOPED — full intelligence profile for assigned
                        students only. Cannot access students outside assignment.
ASSIGNMENT_GATE:        Teacher/Mentor must have active assignment to student
                        in INSTITUTE_SYSTEM or MENTOR_CONTROL_ENGINE
                        Assignment ended → access revoked within 48 hours

MANDATORY_SECTIONS:
  1. Student Intelligence Profile (all 9 dimensions — full scores + confidence)
  2. Pedagogical Strength Map (which intelligence strengths to build upon)
  3. Learning Gap Analysis (dimensions below threshold — with root cause signals)
  4. Learning Velocity Profile (how quickly this student acquires vs. cohort)
  5. Focus & Engagement Profile (consistency, persistence, attention signals)
  6. Debate & Communication Intelligence (DEB dimension detail for verbal coaching)
  7. Creative Intelligence Profile (CRE dimension detail for creative development)
  8. Response Pattern Analysis (OREA patterns — how student constructs answers)
  9. Fallacy Pattern Report (DQAA fallacy detection history — for logical coaching)
  10. Growth Trajectory (timeline + predicted trajectory per dimension)
  11. Intervention Recommendations (3–5 evidence-based teaching interventions)
  12. Peer Cohort Benchmarking (anonymized cohort percentile — no named peers)
  13. Comparison to Prior Session (progress since last report)
  14. Teaching Effectiveness Signals (impact of educator's sessions on student DQI/CDI)
  15. Student Goal Alignment (are the student's goals aligned to intelligence profile?)

SPECIAL_SECTIONS_FOR_MENTOR_MODE:
  16. Argument Map History (DQAA argument maps from mentor-observed sessions)
  17. Pressure Response Profile (ADA dimension detail across all sessions)
  18. Mentor Impact Dashboard (how mentor-observed sessions compare to standard sessions)

LANGUAGE_REGISTER:
  - Professional educator register
  - Evidence-based pedagogical framing
  - Specific, actionable teaching interventions
  - Technical signal detail available (with plain explanation)
  - Comparative context (cohort percentile, benchmark)

FORBIDDEN_IN_TEACHER_REPORT:
  - Personal/private student data (medical, financial, personal circumstances)
  - Individual named peer comparisons
  - Parent-only visible data
  - Hiring recommendations
  - Career prescriptions (educator's role is development, not placement)
  - Data from outside the educator's assigned domain/track

GENERATION_TRIGGER:
  - Educator-requested (on-demand, no monthly cap for certified educators)
  - Scheduled pre-session briefing (24 hours before assigned session)
  - Post-session debrief trigger (automatically after every DQAA/OREA session)
  - Significant learning event (breakthrough, regression alert, belt promotion)
```

---

### Report Type 4 — RECRUITER INTELLIGENCE REPORT (RIR)

```yaml
AUDIENCE:               Verified enterprise recruiters and HR professionals
                        accessing the Ecoskiller Talent Marketplace
PERMISSION_LEVEL:       RECRUITER-SCOPED — curated professional intelligence
                        profile. Excludes all personal, educational, and
                        family-related data. Only professional-relevant signals.
USER_CONSENT_GATE:      User must have opted into recruiter visibility
                        (TALENT_MARKETPLACE_VISIBILITY = ENABLED in user settings)
                        Without explicit opt-in: recruiter cannot access any report
RECRUITER_VERIFICATION: Accessing recruiter must be verified enterprise account
                        in ENTERPRISE_SYSTEM with active hiring access

MANDATORY_SECTIONS:
  1. Professional Intelligence Summary (radar chart — 6 dimensions visible:
     LIN, LOG, CRE, DEB, ITP, LRV — SPA, INP, FOC available with consent)
  2. Skill Intelligence Portfolio (verified skill levels per domain)
  3. Debate Intelligence Profile (DQI — full 7-dimension breakdown)
  4. Creative Intelligence Profile (CDI — full 5-dimension breakdown)
  5. Open Response Quality (OREA-derived communication quality signals)
  6. Learning Velocity Index (how fast this candidate learns vs. talent pool)
  7. Championship & Competition Record (verified competitive performance)
  8. Belt & Certification Record (verified belt progressions and certifications)
  9. Tool-Sync Work Intelligence (real work signals from integrated platforms)
  10. Growth Trajectory (1-year DQI and CDI trend — direction, not just snapshot)
  11. Reliability Score (consistency signal — shows up, completes, follows through)
  12. Career Intelligence Match Score (fit score for role/JD if recruiter provides)
  13. Placement Probability Signal (ML-computed probability for role type)
  14. Peer Percentile (where this candidate stands in domain talent pool)

CAREER_INTELLIGENCE_MATCH:
  If recruiter provides JD or role specification:
    CAREER_AI_AGENT computes:
      - Intelligence profile fit score (0–100)
      - Skill gap analysis (missing vs. required)
      - Growth velocity prediction (time to full role readiness)
    This is advisory signal — NOT a hire/reject recommendation

FORBIDDEN_IN_RECRUITER_REPORT:
  - Age (age discrimination prevention)
  - Personal details (address, phone, parent info)
  - Academic institution names (unless user has opted into sharing)
  - Medical or wellbeing indicators
  - Political or personal beliefs
  - Parent or family-linked data
  - Child-age user data (minors cannot appear in recruiter reports under any condition)
  - Any data where user has set RECRUITER_VISIBILITY = DISABLED
  - Individual named competitors from debate/championship sessions
  - Hiring recommendation (recruiter makes this decision)

MINOR_PROTECTION:
  ABSOLUTE RULE: Users below age 18 CANNOT appear in recruiter reports
  Age verification required before recruiter report generation
  If age_verified = false OR age < 18: REJECT with MINOR_PROTECTION error
  No override permitted. No exception.

GENERATION_TRIGGER:
  - Recruiter-requested (on-demand, subject to rate limit: 100 reports/day per recruiter)
  - Job application event (user applies → RIR auto-generated for recruiter)
  - AI candidate search result (recruiter search → RIR included in result)
  - Saved candidate alert (recruiter-saved candidate reaches new milestone)
```

---

### Report Type 5 — INSTITUTE INTELLIGENCE REPORT (IIR)

```yaml
AUDIENCE:               Institute administrators, placement officers (TPOs),
                        academic leadership, and corporate L&D departments
PERMISSION_LEVEL:       INSTITUTE-SCOPED — aggregated cohort intelligence.
                        Individual user data is NEVER exposed in IIR.
                        All individual signals are aggregated and anonymized.
MINIMUM_COHORT_SIZE:    10 students minimum for any aggregated data point
                        (k-anonymity protection — no cohort smaller than 10)
DATA_SCOPE:             Only students enrolled/linked to the requesting institute
                        Cross-institute comparison: anonymized benchmark only

MANDATORY_SECTIONS:
  1. Cohort Intelligence Overview
     - Average radar chart for cohort (9 dimensions)
     - Percentile distribution per dimension
     - Cohort vs. platform benchmark
  2. Strength & Gap Analysis (cohort-level strengths and development zones)
  3. Intelligence Tier Distribution
     (% DISTINGUISHED / ACCOMPLISHED / DEVELOPING / NOVICE per dimension)
  4. Learning Velocity Cohort Profile
     (cohort avg vs. benchmark — are students learning fast or slow?)
  5. Creativity Intelligence Distribution (CDI distribution for cohort)
  6. Debate Intelligence Distribution (DQI distribution for cohort)
  7. Placement Intelligence Signals
     (recruiter engagement rate, placement probability distribution,
      belt/certification completion rate for cohort)
  8. Domain Track Breakdown (intelligence profile per domain_track)
  9. Championship & Competition Participation Rate
  10. Cohort Growth Trajectory (month-over-month intelligence improvement)
  11. High-Potential Signal (% of cohort crossing BREAKTHROUGH threshold — no names)
  12. At-Risk Signal (% of cohort below minimum thresholds — no names — for intervention)
  13. Institute Effectiveness Score (how much intelligence growth correlates
      with institute-delivered sessions — teaching effectiveness signal)
  14. Benchmark Comparison (anonymized peer institute comparison where available)
  15. Recommendations for Institute (3–5 curriculum or coaching interventions)

K_ANONYMITY_ENFORCEMENT:
  Any data point that could identify an individual within the cohort:
    → SUPPRESS and substitute with "Insufficient cohort size for this breakdown"
  Cohort segments < 10 users: NOT reported
  Example: "Arts track females, age 18–19" = likely < 10 in small institute → SUPPRESSED

INDIVIDUAL_DATA_EXPOSURE:
  ABSOLUTE PROHIBITION — IIR never exposes individual user data
  No user_id, no name, no identifier appears in any IIR
  Violation → SECURITY_CRITICAL incident

GENERATION_TRIGGER:
  - Institute admin request (on-demand, max 2 full reports per day)
  - Monthly scheduled auto-generation (1st of each month)
  - Academic semester end trigger
  - Pre-placement season trigger (configurable per institute)
  - Cohort milestone event (significant % of cohort crossing threshold)
```

## ▶ SECTION 5 — INPUT CONTRACT (STRICT)

### 5.1 Input Schema

```json
INPUT_SCHEMA: {
  "required_fields": [
    "report_request_id",
    "requesting_actor_id",
    "requesting_actor_role",
    "target_entity_id",
    "target_entity_type",
    "report_type",
    "tenant_id",
    "domain_scope",
    "session_token",
    "report_generation_mode",
    "report_version_requested",
    "consent_verification_reference"
  ],
  "optional_fields": [
    "age_band_override",
    "report_period_start",
    "report_period_end",
    "localization_code",
    "job_description_for_match",
    "specific_dimensions_requested",
    "include_career_mapping",
    "include_predictions",
    "cohort_filter_params",
    "comparison_benchmark_type",
    "accessibility_mode_flag",
    "delivery_channel"
  ],

  "field_definitions": {
    "report_request_id":          "UUID-v4 — unique request identifier",
    "requesting_actor_id":        "UUID-v4 — actor requesting the report",
    "requesting_actor_role":      "enum [STUDENT_SELF, PARENT, TEACHER, MENTOR, RECRUITER, INSTITUTE_ADMIN, PLATFORM_ADMIN, COMPLIANCE_OFFICER]",
    "target_entity_id":           "UUID-v4 — user_id (individual) or cohort_id (institute)",
    "target_entity_type":         "enum [INDIVIDUAL_USER, INSTITUTE_COHORT, DOMAIN_COHORT]",
    "report_type":                "enum [SIR, PIR, TMIR, RIR, IIR]",
    "tenant_id":                  "UUID-v4 — must match session_token claims",
    "domain_scope":               "enum [Arts, Commerce, Science, Technology, Administration, ALL]",
    "session_token":              "JWT — validated, non-expired, tenant-bound",
    "report_generation_mode":     "enum [on_demand, scheduled, event_triggered, batch]",
    "report_version_requested":   "string — IRGA report schema version",
    "consent_verification_reference": "UUID — verified consent record for data access",

    "report_period_start":        "ISO-8601 date — report covers data from this date",
    "report_period_end":          "ISO-8601 date — report covers data to this date",
    "localization_code":          "ISO 639-1 — report language and cultural calibration",
    "job_description_for_match":  "string — JD text for RIR career match computation (max 10000 chars)",
    "specific_dimensions_requested": ["array of dimension codes — if null, all dimensions included"],
    "cohort_filter_params":       "object — for IIR: domain_track, age_band, enrollment_date_range",
    "comparison_benchmark_type":  "enum [PLATFORM_GLOBAL, DOMAIN_COHORT, INSTITUTE_COHORT, NONE]",
    "delivery_channel":           "enum [DASHBOARD_RENDER, PDF_EXPORT, EMAIL_DELIVERY, API_RESPONSE]"
  },

  "validation_rules": [
    "report_request_id must be UUID-v4",
    "requesting_actor_id must be active verified user in tenant",
    "requesting_actor_role must be in allowed enum",
    "target_entity_id must exist and be accessible to requesting_actor",
    "report_type must match requesting_actor_role permission matrix (Section 5.2)",
    "tenant_id must match session_token claims",
    "domain_scope must be in allowed enum",
    "report_generation_mode must be in allowed enum",
    "consent_verification_reference must resolve as valid and active consent record",
    "if report_type = RIR: target user must be age >= 18 (minor protection)",
    "if report_type = PIR: requesting_actor must have verified parent link to target_user",
    "if report_type = TMIR: requesting_actor must have active assignment to target_user",
    "if report_type = IIR: target_entity_type must be INSTITUTE_COHORT or DOMAIN_COHORT",
    "if report_type = IIR: cohort size must be >= 10 (k-anonymity enforcement)",
    "report_period dates must be valid ISO-8601 and report_period_start <= report_period_end",
    "if specific_dimensions_requested is not null: all codes must be in valid dimension enum",
    "job_description_for_match only valid when report_type = RIR"
  ],

  "permission_matrix": {
    "STUDENT_SELF":       ["SIR"],
    "PARENT":             ["PIR"],
    "TEACHER":            ["TMIR"],
    "MENTOR":             ["TMIR"],
    "RECRUITER":          ["RIR"],
    "INSTITUTE_ADMIN":    ["IIR", "TMIR (own institute students only)"],
    "PLATFORM_ADMIN":     ["SIR", "PIR", "TMIR", "RIR", "IIR (compliance mode)"],
    "COMPLIANCE_OFFICER": ["ALL (audit mode — flagged in audit trail)"]
  },

  "security_checks": [
    "Tenant isolation: target_entity must belong to requesting_actor's tenant",
    "Cross-tenant report access: FORBIDDEN — REJECT + LOG_SECURITY_CRITICAL",
    "Session token: JWT validation + expiry + scope",
    "Consent: consent_verification_reference must be active and cover requested data scope",
    "Minor protection: RIR for users < 18 = REJECT + LOG_MINOR_PROTECTION_VIOLATION",
    "Parent link: PIR requires verified parent-child link in TRUST_SYSTEM",
    "Assignment gate: TMIR requires active assignment in INSTITUTE or MENTOR system",
    "Rate limiting: see Section 4 per report type",
    "RBAC: requesting actor must hold role matching permission_matrix",
    "PII in job_description: scan and flag if JD contains PII before processing",
    "IIR k-anonymity: cohort size enforcement at query layer, not output layer"
  ],

  "data_minimization_rules": [
    "SIR: Only data from report_period_start to report_period_end",
    "PIR: Only dimensions visible per child's privacy settings",
    "TMIR: Only data from sessions assigned to requesting educator",
    "RIR: Only recruiter-visible fields per user consent settings",
    "IIR: Aggregated only — no individual user data fields in any query"
  ]
}
```

### 5.2 Minimum Signal Threshold Policy

```yaml
MINIMUM_SIGNALS_REQUIRED_PER_DIMENSION:
  LIN:  3 distinct source signals
  LOG:  3 distinct source signals
  SPA:  2 distinct source signals
  ITP:  3 distinct source signals
  INP:  3 distinct source signals
  CRE:  CDA composite_cdi mandatory (no minimum count override)
  DEB:  DQAA composite_dqi mandatory (no minimum count override)
  LRV:  3 distinct source signals + 30-day data window
  FOC:  3 distinct source signals + 14-day behavioral window

INSUFFICIENT_DATA_POLICY:
  If a dimension does not meet minimum signal threshold:
    - Dimension score: NULL (not computed)
    - Radar axis: GRAYED OUT in visualization
    - Report section: "Insufficient activity to measure this dimension yet"
    - Recommendation: Specific activities that would generate signals for this dimension
    - No dimension is shown as 0 — 0 and NULL are distinct states
    - A NULL dimension is NOT included in composite intelligence score

COMPOSITE_INTELLIGENCE_SCORE:
  Computed only from dimensions with sufficient data (non-NULL)
  Minimum 5 dimensions required for composite score computation
  If < 5 dimensions have data: composite = NULL; report still generated
```

### 5.3 Validation Failure Protocol

```
ON VALIDATION FAILURE:
  1. STOP_EXECUTION immediately — no partial report produced
  2. LOG: validation_failure_event (append-only) → GOVERNANCE_AGENT
  3. EMIT: error_event → OBSERVABILITY_AGENT
  4. RETURN: structured error payload {failure_code, field_path, remediation_hint}
  5. ESCALATE: if failure_code = SECURITY or MINOR_PROTECTION → COMPLIANCE_AGENT
  6. NO silent failures under any condition
```

}

### 6.3 Output Guarantees

- `audit_reference` MANDATORY in every report — no anonymous report generation
- `consent_reference` MANDATORY — report not generated without verified consent record
- `report_confidence.overall_confidence` MANDATORY — no confidence-blind reports
- Intelligence radar chart data MANDATORY even if all dimensions NULL (shows data gaps)
- Partial section outputs FORBIDDEN — all-or-nothing per report type
- Minor protection check: if target user age < 18 AND report_type = RIR → REJECT
- IIR k-anonymity enforcement: any data point < 10 users → suppress before output construction
- LLM-generated narrative text must be ≤ 600 chars and flagged as AI_GENERATED in output
- Career mapping is clearly labeled as ADVISORY — not a hiring or rejection recommendation

---

## ▶ SECTION 7 — ML / AI LOGIC LAYER

### 7.1 Architecture Split (Enforced)

```
75% Traditional ML — COMPUTATION & SYNTHESIS LAYER:
  - Dimension score computation (weighted average with calibration)
  - Signal normalization (Z-score → percentile → 0–100 scale per domain)
  - Composite intelligence score computation
  - Growth trajectory computation (regression on historical signals)
  - Intelligence prediction models (30/90/180-day trajectory)
  - Career intelligence match scoring (if JD provided)
  - Placement probability model
  - Reliability score computation
  - Learning velocity computation
  - Alert threshold detection (rule-based + ML anomaly detection)
  - Cohort aggregation models (for IIR)
  - Drift detection on dimension distributions

25% LLM / Semantic Reasoning — NARRATIVE & ADVISORY LAYER:
  - Growth narrative generation (audience-appropriate, max 600 chars)
  - Strength narrative (why these are strengths — evidence-referenced)
  - Growth zone framing (age-appropriate, constructive language)
  - Career pathway description (what roles and why for this profile)
  - Intervention recommendations (for TMIR — teaching strategies)
  - Intelligence alert descriptions (audience-specific language)
  - Institute recommendation generation (for IIR — curriculum interventions)
  - Age-band register adaptation (re-register of content for child vs. adult)
```

> **ABSOLUTE RULE:** LLM generates narrative and advisory text ONLY.
> LLM NEVER computes dimension scores.
> LLM NEVER modifies intelligence scores from ML computation.
> LLM NEVER generates career prescriptions — only descriptions.
> LLM output is always labeled AI_GENERATED and confidence-flagged.
> LLM timeout → report generated with ML data only; narrative = "Generating..."
> (deferred async retry for narrative sections).

### 7.2 Intelligence Prediction Models

```yaml
TRAJECTORY_PREDICTION:
  model_type:         Time-series regression (ARIMA-inspired, domain-calibrated)
  prediction_windows: 30 days | 90 days | 180 days
  per_dimension:      YES — separate prediction per dimension
  confidence_band:    Prediction includes upper/lower confidence interval
  minimum_history:    6 data points per dimension for prediction
  below_minimum:      Prediction = null (not computed)
  staleness_check:    If most recent signal > 60 days old → prediction flagged STALE

CAREER_INTELLIGENCE_MATCH:
  model_type:         Multi-label classification + similarity scoring
  input:              Intelligence profile vector + JD text embedding
  output:             Match score (0–100) + top 3 matching role archetypes
                      + skill gap analysis + velocity-to-readiness estimate
  advisory_label:     REQUIRED — output labeled "AI Advisory Signal" in all reports
  human_decision:     Hiring decision remains with recruiter — ENFORCED

PLACEMENT_PROBABILITY_MODEL:
  model_type:         Regression trained on historical placement outcome data
  input:              Intelligence profile + skill profile + domain + role type
  output:             Probability score (0–1) + confidence
  minimum_data:       Sufficient labeled placement outcomes in domain
  training_frequency: Quarterly (placement data lags — less frequent than eval models)
  advisory_label:     REQUIRED

COHORT_INTELLIGENCE_MODEL (IIR):
  model_type:         Descriptive statistics + distribution modeling
  input:              Aggregated (anonymized) dimension scores for cohort
  output:             Distribution, percentile bands, tier breakdown, growth rates
  k_anonymity:        Enforced at query layer — no individual data in model input
```

### 7.3 LLM Governance

```yaml
LLM_USAGE_SCOPE:
  permitted:
    - growth_narrative_generation (audience-specific, max 600 chars)
    - strength_narrative_text (evidence-referenced, max 400 chars)
    - growth_zone_framing (constructive language, audience-adapted)
    - career_pathway_descriptions (descriptive, not prescriptive, max 300 chars each)
    - teaching_intervention_recommendations (TMIR only, structured format)
    - intelligence_alert_descriptions (audience-appropriate language)
    - institute_recommendations (IIR only, curriculum-level)
    - age_band_register_adaptation (re-registering content for CHILD age band)

  forbidden:
    - Computing any intelligence dimension score
    - Modifying ML-computed scores
    - Making career prescriptions ("You MUST become X")
    - Accessing user identity details in context window
    - Cross-tenant data access
    - Generating hiring or rejection recommendations
    - Producing diagnostic medical or psychological assessments
    - Generating content for IIR that could identify individuals

PROMPT_GOVERNANCE:
  all_prompts_version_tagged:  YES (irga_prompt_version in every output)
  temperature:                 0.2 for narrative / 0.0 for structured sections
  output_format:               Structured JSON with narrative fields clearly labeled
  max_tokens_per_section:      800 per narrative section
  prompt_injection_defense:    All input signals sanitized before LLM context assembly
  context_isolation:           LLM receives aggregated scores + dimension labels
                               NO raw transcript content, NO named users (TMIR/IIR),
                               NO cross-tenant data
  llm_timeout_fallback:        Report generated with ML data only;
                               narrative sections = "Report narrative generating..."
                               + async retry queued

AI_GENERATED_LABELING:
  all_llm_output:              Labeled AI_GENERATED in output schema
  consumer_disclosure:         All report renders must surface AI_GENERATED label
                               for any LLM-produced text (transparency requirement)
```

---

## ▶ SECTION 8 — INTELLIGENCE ALERT SYSTEM

```yaml
ALERT_TYPES:

  DIMENSION_BREAKTHROUGH:
    trigger:          Any dimension increases by >= 15 points in 30 days
    severity:         INFO
    audience:         SIR, PIR, TMIR
    action:           Badge check, parent notification, growth achievement trigger

  DIMENSION_REGRESSION_ALERT:
    trigger:          Any dimension decreases by >= 10 points in 30 days
    severity:         ATTENTION
    audience:         SIR, TMIR (not PIR by default — configurable)
    action:           Intervention recommendation generated in TMIR

  CREATIVE_EMERGENCE:
    trigger:          CRE dimension crosses INNOVATOR band for first time
    severity:         INFO
    audience:         SIR, PIR (for child users), TMIR
    action:           Creative achievement badge check, mentor notification

  DEBATE_MILESTONE:
    trigger:          DEB dimension crosses ACCOMPLISHED band for first time
    severity:         INFO
    audience:         SIR, TMIR, RIR (if talent marketplace opted in)
    action:           Debate achievement badge check

  CAREER_READINESS_SIGNAL:
    trigger:          Composite intelligence score crosses 70.0
                      AND minimum 5 dimensions computed
                      AND age >= 18
    severity:         INFO
    audience:         SIR, RIR (if opted in)
    action:           Career intelligence map update trigger

  LOW_SIGNAL_ALERT:
    trigger:          < 5 dimensions have sufficient data after 60 days of activity
    severity:         ATTENTION
    audience:         SIR, TMIR
    action:           Specific activity recommendations to generate missing signals

  DATA_STALENESS_ALERT:
    trigger:          Most recent signal for any dimension > 90 days old
    severity:         ATTENTION (for TMIR) / INFO (for SIR)
    audience:         SIR, TMIR
    action:           Prompt for re-evaluation activity

  FOCUS_DEGRADATION_ALERT:
    trigger:          FOC dimension drops >= 12 points in 21 days
    severity:         ATTENTION
    audience:         TMIR only (not surfaced to student or parent by default)
    action:           Educator intervention recommendation

  RECRUITER_VISIBILITY_MILESTONE:
    trigger:          User's DQI or CDI crosses top 10th percentile in domain
    severity:         INFO
    audience:         SIR only
    action:           Prompt user to enable talent marketplace visibility
                      (cannot auto-enable — explicit user action required)
```

---

## ▶ SECTION 9 — SCALABILITY DESIGN

```yaml
EXPECTED_REPORT_VOLUME:     5M reports/day at 100M users (10M–100M scale)
                            Peak: 10M reports/day (month-end, semester-end cycles)

LATENCY_TARGETS:
  on_demand_SIR:            p95 < 4 seconds
  on_demand_PIR:            p95 < 5 seconds
  on_demand_TMIR:           p95 < 6 seconds
  on_demand_RIR:            p95 < 5 seconds
  on_demand_IIR:            p95 < 12 seconds (cohort aggregation is compute-intensive)
  scheduled_batch:          Best-effort within 2-hour window
  event_triggered:          p95 < 8 seconds

MAX_CONCURRENCY:            20,000 concurrent report generation jobs

QUEUE_STRATEGY:
  on_demand_priority:       CRITICAL lane (user waiting)
  event_triggered:          HIGH lane
  scheduled_monthly:        STANDARD lane (off-peak hours)
  batch_export:             BATCH lane (nights/weekends)

CACHING_POLICY:
  SIR:                      Cache 6 hours (invalidated on new evaluation signal)
  PIR:                      Cache 6 hours (invalidated on new signal or child activity)
  TMIR:                     Cache 2 hours (educators need fresh data)
  RIR:                      Cache 12 hours (recruiter view, less time-sensitive)
  IIR:                      Cache 24 hours (cohort data changes slowly)
  cache_key:                {report_type}:{target_entity_id}:{tenant_id}:{report_period}

IDEMPOTENCY:
  key:                      report_request_id
  behavior:                 Duplicate requests return cached result if within TTL
  TTL:                      Per report type cache policy above

REPORT_PRE_GENERATION:
  scheduled_reports:        Pre-generated during off-peak hours (2 AM – 6 AM tenant TZ)
  event_triggered:          Pre-generated within 60 seconds of triggering event
  on_demand:                Generated at request time (cached result returned if fresh)

SCALING_MODE:               Horizontal auto-scaling, stateless workers per report_type
RETRY_POLICY:               3 retries, exponential backoff (2s → 8s → 32s)
DEAD_LETTER_QUEUE:          Failed generations → DLQ + alert OBSERVABILITY_AGENT
```

---

## ▶ SECTION 10 — SECURITY ENFORCEMENT

```yaml
TENANT_ISOLATION:
  enforcement:          RLS at database layer + application layer
  cross_tenant:         ABSOLUTE PROHIBITION — REJECT + LOG_SECURITY_CRITICAL
  cohort_isolation:     IIR cohort queries scoped to tenant_id + institute_id

CONSENT_ENFORCEMENT:
  every_report:         Consent record must be active and cover data scope
  consent_revocation:   If user revokes consent mid-generation → abort + LOG
  consent_expiry:       Expired consent → report generation blocked → LOG_CONSENT_EXPIRED
  recruiter_consent:    RIR generation requires TALENT_MARKETPLACE_VISIBILITY = ENABLED
                        per user settings at generation time (not at consent grant time)

MINOR_PROTECTION:
  RIR:                  ABSOLUTE — no report for user age < 18
  PIR:                  Requires verified parent-child link
  SIR for minors:       Available — own data, age-adapted
  TMIR for minors:      Available — assigned educator only
  IIR:                  Minors included in cohort aggregate only — never individually identifiable

PRIVACY_ARCHITECTURE:
  data_minimization:    Each report type receives only the data it is permitted to access
  field_level_security: Per-field access control on FEATURE_STORE queries
  LLM_context:          No identifiable user data in LLM context (aggregated scores only)
  IIR_query:            Aggregated query — individual user data never materializes in memory

ENCRYPTION:
  report_at_rest:       AES-256
  report_in_transit:    TLS 1.3 minimum
  audit_logs:           Encrypted + append-only

ACCESS_LOG:
  per_generation:       requesting_actor_id, target_entity_id, report_type,
                        tenant_id, input_hash, output_hash, timestamp
  log_store:            Append-only — no UPDATE, no DELETE

REPORT_WATERMARKING:
  RIR:                  Digital watermark embedded in PDF exports (requesting_actor_id)
                        for leak detection
  IIR:                  Watermarked with institute_id and generation_timestamp
  purpose:              Audit trail for unauthorized redistribution detection

SCREENSHOT_POLICY:
  RIR_DETAIL_SCREENS:   Screenshot/recording blocked on sensitive candidate detail screens
  aligned_with:         Flutter UI SCREENSHOT_BLOCKING for SENSITIVE_SCREENS
```

---

## ▶ SECTION 11 — AUDIT & TRACEABILITY

Every report generation MUST produce the following immutable audit record:

```json
AUDIT_RECORD: {
  "audit_id":                       "UUID-v4",
  "timestamp_utc":                  "ISO-8601",
  "actor_id":                       "requesting_actor_id",
  "actor_role":                     "requesting_actor_role",
  "tenant_id":                      "UUID",
  "target_entity_id":               "UUID",
  "target_entity_type":             "string",
  "report_type":                    "string",
  "report_id":                      "UUID (output)",
  "report_period":                  {"start": "ISO-8601", "end": "ISO-8601"},
  "consent_reference":              "UUID",
  "consent_verified":               "boolean",
  "minor_protection_check":         "PASSED or REJECTED",
  "input_hash":                     "SHA-256 of serialized input",
  "output_hash":                    "SHA-256 of serialized report",
  "model_version":                  "string",
  "irga_prompt_version":            "string",
  "dimensions_computed":            "integer",
  "dimensions_insufficient":        "integer",
  "llm_calls_made":                 "integer",
  "llm_available":                  "boolean",
  "ai_generated_sections":          ["array of section names with LLM output"],
  "cache_hit":                      "boolean",
  "generation_duration_ms":         "integer",
  "delivery_channel":               "string",
  "downstream_events_emitted":      ["array"],
  "data_staleness_days":            "integer",
  "k_anonymity_suppressions":       "integer (IIR only)"
}
```

**Audit Policy:**
- IMMUTABLE — append-only, no UPDATE, no DELETE
- Encrypted at rest — tenant-isolated
- Retention: minimum 7 years (enterprise compliance)
- Recruiter report audit: flagged separately (TALENT_ACCESS audit class)
- AI-generated content: all LLM sections logged separately
- Export: via COMPLIANCE_AGENT API for authorized roles

---

## ▶ SECTION 12 — FAILURE POLICY

```yaml
FAILURE_SCENARIOS:

  INVALID_INPUT:
    action:             STOP_EXECUTION
    log:                LOG_VALIDATION_INCIDENT
    escalate:           GOVERNANCE_AGENT
    retry:              NO
    response:           Structured error {failure_code, field_path, remediation_hint}

  CONSENT_MISSING_OR_EXPIRED:
    action:             STOP_EXECUTION
    log:                LOG_CONSENT_FAILURE
    escalate:           PRIVACY_AGENT
    retry:              NO (consent must be re-established by user action)
    response:           CONSENT_REQUIRED error with consent collection link

  MINOR_PROTECTION_VIOLATION:
    action:             IMMEDIATE STOP_EXECUTION
    log:                LOG_MINOR_PROTECTION_VIOLATION (CRITICAL class)
    escalate:           COMPLIANCE_AGENT + LEGAL_TEAM
    retry:              NO — never

  INSUFFICIENT_DATA_ALL_DIMENSIONS:
    action:             PRODUCE EMPTY REPORT with guidance
    behavior:           Radar chart shows all dimensions as INSUFFICIENT_DATA
    sections:           Only "Data Gaps" section + activity recommendations
    log:                LOG_INSUFFICIENT_DATA
    downstream:         No intelligence alerts, no career mapping, no growth narrative

  FEATURE_STORE_UNAVAILABLE:
    action:             STOP_EXECUTION (cannot produce report without data)
    log:                LOG_DATA_SOURCE_UNAVAILABLE
    escalate:           OBSERVABILITY_AGENT + ENGINEERING_ONCALL
    retry:              YES (3 retries, exponential backoff)
    fallback:           If retry exhausted → serve cached report if < 24h old

  LLM_TIMEOUT:
    action:             PROCEED without narrative sections
    behavior:           ML data sections complete; narrative = "Generating..."
    flag:               AI_PARTIAL_MODE in output
    async_retry:        Narrative generation retried async; report updated when ready
    log:                LOG_LLM_TIMEOUT

  CAREER_AI_AGENT_UNAVAILABLE:
    action:             PROCEED without career mapping section
    behavior:           career_intelligence_map.enabled = false
    flag:               CAREER_MAP_UNAVAILABLE in output
    log:                LOG_CAREER_AGENT_UNAVAILABLE

  K_ANONYMITY_ENFORCEMENT_FAILURE (IIR):
    action:             STOP_EXECUTION — never produce IIR that could de-anonymize
    log:                LOG_K_ANONYMITY_FAILURE (CRITICAL)
    escalate:           PRIVACY_AGENT + COMPLIANCE_AGENT
    retry:              NO

  TENANT_ISOLATION_VIOLATION:
    action:             IMMEDIATE STOP_EXECUTION
    log:                LOG_SECURITY_CRITICAL
    escalate:           COMPLIANCE_AGENT + SECURITY_TEAM (P0 incident)
    retry:              NO

  CACHE_CORRUPTION:
    action:             INVALIDATE cache, regenerate fresh
    log:                LOG_CACHE_CORRUPTION
    retry:              YES (fresh generation)

NO_SILENT_FAILURES:     ABSOLUTE — every failure path produces a log entry
```

---

## ▶ SECTION 13 — INTER-AGENT DEPENDENCY MAP & EVENTS

### 13.1 Upstream Dependencies

```
FEATURE_STORE_AGENT             → all dimension signal vectors (primary data source)
OREA                            → open response competency signals
CDA                             → creativity divergence vectors (CRE dimension primary)
DQAA                            → debate quality vectors (DEB dimension primary)
BELT_ENGINE                     → belt records, promotion history
RATING_ENGINE                   → match ratings per domain
GROWTH_ENGINE                   → XP, streaks, achievement records
SKILL_ENGINE                    → skill level records
ADAPTIVE_TESTING_ENGINE         → intelligence test scores per dimension
CHAMPIONSHIP_ENGINE             → championship records
INTEGRATION_ENGINE              → tool-sync skill signals
CAREER_AI_AGENT                 → career pathway recommendations
MENTOR_CONTROL_ENGINE           → structured mentor observations
PASSIVE_INTELLIGENCE_PIPELINE   → behavioral intelligence signals
TRUST_SYSTEM                    → parent-child link verification (PIR)
PRIVACY_AGENT                   → consent verification, user privacy settings
```

### 13.2 Downstream Dependencies

```
NOTIFICATION_SERVICE            ← report ready alerts per audience
STUDENT_DASHBOARD               ← SIR render
PARENT_DASHBOARD                ← PIR render
TEACHER_MENTOR_DASHBOARD        ← TMIR render
RECRUITER_SYSTEM                ← RIR render, candidate intelligence signal
INSTITUTE_ANALYTICS_ENGINE      ← IIR render
GOVERNANCE_AGENT                ← audit records per generation
OBSERVABILITY_AGENT             ← metrics, errors, latency
CAREER_AI_AGENT                 ← refreshed intelligence profile trigger
GROWTH_ENGINE                   ← report milestone badge check
ALERT_ENGINE                    ← intelligence alert emission
```

### 13.3 Event Triggers

```yaml
REPORT_GENERATED_EVENT:
  always_emitted:       true
  payload:              report_id, report_type, target_entity_id, tenant_id,
                        generation_timestamp, delivery_channel

INTELLIGENCE_ALERT_EVENT:
  condition:            alert_type triggered per Section 8 rules
  payload:              alert_type, severity, dimension_code, target_entity_id,
                        audience_types, recommended_action

CAREER_MAP_UPDATE_TRIGGER:
  condition:            SIR generated + composite_intelligence_score >= 70.0
                        + age >= 18
  payload:              user_id, intelligence_profile_vector → CAREER_AI_AGENT

REPORT_MILESTONE_EVENT:
  condition:            First SIR generated / First IIR generated / etc.
  payload:              user_id, report_type → GROWTH_ENGINE (badge check)

RECRUITER_ACCESS_LOG_EVENT:
  condition:            RIR generated and delivered
  payload:              recruiter_id, target_user_id, timestamp → AUDIT trail
                        User notified (configurable per privacy settings)

INSTITUTE_BENCHMARK_UPDATE_EVENT:
  condition:            IIR generated
  payload:              institute_id, cohort_size, avg_composite_score,
                        benchmark_comparison → INSTITUTE_ANALYTICS_ENGINE
```

---

## ▶ SECTION 14 — PASSIVE INTELLIGENCE COMPATIBILITY

```yaml
FEATURE_VECTOR_EMISSION:
  Note:    IRGA is primarily a CONSUMER of feature vectors from FEATURE_STORE_AGENT
           It does NOT emit new evaluation signals (those come from upstream agents)
           IRGA emits REPORT_GENERATED signals and INTELLIGENCE_PROFILE_SNAPSHOT

INTELLIGENCE_PROFILE_SNAPSHOT_EMISSION:
  trigger:          Every SIR generation (on-demand or scheduled)
  destination:      FEATURE_STORE_AGENT
  payload:
    user_id:             UUID
    snapshot_type:       "intelligence_profile_snapshot"
    all_dimension_scores: object (all computed dimensions)
    composite_score:     float
    intelligence_tier:   string
    timestamp:           generation_timestamp_utc
    model_version:       string
  purpose:          Enables CAREER_AI_AGENT and RECRUITER_SYSTEM to use
                    pre-computed profile vectors without re-fetching raw signals
```

## ▶ SECTION 15 — GROWTH ENGINE HOOK

```yaml
REPORT_ACHIEVEMENT_TRIGGERS:
  - First SIR generated → "First Intelligence Report" milestone
  - First all-9-dimensions computed → "Full Intelligence Unlocked" badge
  - Composite intelligence score first crosses 70 → "Intelligence Leader" badge check
  - EXCEPTIONAL tier first reached → "Exceptional Talent" badge
  - Career intelligence map generated first time → "Career Clarity" milestone
  - First IIR generated (institute admin) → "Cohort Intelligence Activated" milestone

INTELLIGENCE_GOAL_TRACKING:
  SIR includes:     3 SMART-structured intelligence goals generated from profile
  goal_types:
    DIMENSION_GOAL:     "Improve LIN score from X to Y within 90 days"
    SIGNAL_GOAL:        "Complete 3 DQAA evaluations to unlock DEB dimension"
    CAREER_GOAL:        "Achieve ACCOMPLISHED debate band to qualify for X role type"
  tracking:         Goal progress checked on each subsequent SIR generation
  growth_engine:    Goal completion triggers XP award + milestone notification
```

## ▶ SECTION 16 — CAREER INTELLIGENCE MAPPING

```yaml
CAREER_MAP_ARCHITECTURE:
  model_type:         Collaborative filtering + profile-based recommendation
  input:              Nine-dimension intelligence profile vector
                      + domain_track + skill_levels + belt_records
  output:             Top 5 career pathways with:
                        - role_archetype
                        - match_score (0–100)
                        - reasoning_narrative (LLM-generated, max 200 chars)
                        - skill_gap_analysis (missing skills for role)
                        - velocity_to_readiness (estimated time at current LRV)
                        - ecoskiller_pathway (how platform skills map to role)

CAREER_PREDICTION_HORIZON:
  default:            180 days
  configurable:       YES (per user preference, 30 / 90 / 180 days)

ADVISORY_LABELING:
  all_career_output:  Labeled "AI Advisory — Not a guarantee or professional advice"
  IRGA_rule:          Career mapping is descriptive and probabilistic — not prescriptive
  human_agency:       User always makes career decisions — platform advises only

CAREER_MAP_SOURCES:
  CAREER_AI_AGENT:    Primary computation (IRGA calls CAREER_AI_AGENT API)
  SKILL_ENGINE:       Skill-to-role mapping contribution
  BELT_ENGINE:        Certification-to-career alignment
  CHAMPIONSHIP:       Competitive performance as career signal
  PLACEMENT_MODEL:    Probability estimates for role types

INDUSTRY_DEMAND_INTEGRATION:
  CAREER_AI_AGENT:    Integrates real-time industry demand signals (where available)
  demand_weighting:   High-demand roles surfaced higher in recommendations
  source:             Platform-aggregated recruiter search data (anonymized)

CAREER_MAP_DOMAINS:
  Arts track:         Creative direction, content strategy, media, design, fine arts
  Commerce track:     Finance, sales, HR, supply chain, consulting, entrepreneurship
  Science track:      Research, medicine, engineering, data science, academia
  Technology track:   Software, AI/ML, cybersecurity, product management, DevOps
  Administration:     Policy, governance, public affairs, law, social impact
```

## ▶ SECTION 17 — INSTITUTE INTELLIGENCE ENGINE INTEGRATION

```yaml
INSTITUTE_EFFECTIVENESS_SCORE:
  definition:         Correlation between institute-delivered sessions and
                      student intelligence growth rates (LRV contribution)
  computation:        Cohort DQI/CDI growth rate (sessions with institute mentor)
                      vs. cohort growth rate (self-directed sessions)
  output:             Effectiveness multiplier (1.0 = neutral, > 1.0 = positive)
  use_case:           Institute quality signal for platform ranking (where applicable)
  minimum_cohort:     30 students minimum for effectiveness score

COHORT_INTELLIGENCE_BENCHMARKING:
  platform_benchmark:         Anonymized average across all platform users in domain
  peer_institute_benchmark:   Anonymized average of similar-sized institutes
                              (institute type classification required)
  no_institute_identification: Benchmark data never reveals which institutes are included

PLACEMENT_INTELLIGENCE_FOR_INSTITUTES:
  recruiter_engagement_rate:  % of cohort appearing in recruiter searches
  placement_probability_avg:  Average placement probability for cohort
  belt_certification_rate:    % of cohort achieving required certifications
  top_candidate_signal:       % of cohort in top 20th percentile (no names — count only)

TPO_DASHBOARD_INTEGRATION:
  IIR feeds:          Institute placement officer dashboard (Module 6 integration)
  report_frequency:   Monthly full IIR + real-time cohort snapshot
  TPO_actions:        Intervention alerts, placement readiness signals, skill gap flags
```

## ▶ SECTION 18 — PERFORMANCE MONITORING

```yaml
METRICS_EMITTED_TO_OBSERVABILITY_AGENT:

  report_generation_success_rate:
    target:           >= 99.5%
    alert:            < 99.0%

  latency_p95_by_type:
    SIR_target:       4000ms
    PIR_target:       5000ms
    TMIR_target:      6000ms
    RIR_target:       5000ms
    IIR_target:       12000ms
    alert:            > 150% of target for any type

  cache_hit_rate:
    target:           >= 60% (scheduled + event-triggered)
    alert:            < 40%

  consent_failure_rate:
    target:           < 2%
    alert:            > 5% → privacy system review

  minor_protection_violation_rate:
    target:           0% (absolute)
    alert:            ANY occurrence → P0 incident

  llm_timeout_rate:
    alert:            > 5% → LLM infrastructure review

  insufficient_data_rate:
    definition:       % of SIRs with < 5 dimensions computed
    monitor:          By domain, by user cohort (new users expected high rate)
    alert:            > 30% for users with > 60 days activity → signal gap review

  k_anonymity_suppression_rate (IIR):
    definition:       % of IIR data points suppressed for k-anonymity
    monitor:          High rate may indicate very small institutes
    alert:            > 40% → IIR may not be useful; notify institute admin

  career_map_generation_rate:
    definition:       % of SIRs (adult, opted-in) including career map
    alert:            < 70% when CAREER_AI_AGENT available → integration review

  model_drift_per_dimension:
    metric:           PSI on dimension score distributions
    threshold:        PSI > 0.2 per dimension → retraining review
    frequency:        Weekly batch

DASHBOARDS:
  - Report generation volume by type and tenant
  - Average intelligence dimension scores by domain (platform-wide)
  - Dimension NULL rate (signal gap visibility)
  - Consent failure rate
  - Latency distribution by report type
  - Career map generation rate
  - Intelligence tier distribution
  - Alert firing rate by type
  - LLM availability and quality metrics
  - IIR k-anonymity suppression rate
```

---

## ▶ SECTION 19 — VERSIONING POLICY

```yaml
VERSION_FORMAT:         Semantic Versioning (MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH)
CURRENT_VERSION:        v1.0.0

MUTATION_POLICY:        ADD-ONLY (all changes must be non-destructive)

CHANGE_GOVERNANCE:

  ALLOWED_WITHOUT_VERSION_BUMP:
    - Add new optional output field
    - Add new intelligence alert type
    - Add new career pathway category
    - Add new achievement trigger
    - Monitoring threshold adjustment
    - LLM narrative text improvement (same structure)

  REQUIRES_MINOR_VERSION_BUMP:
    - New optional input field
    - New report section (non-breaking)
    - New delivery channel
    - New career domain track
    - New age band category

  REQUIRES_MAJOR_VERSION_BUMP + GOVERNANCE_APPROVAL:
    - Add or remove intelligence dimension
    - Change dimension weight table
    - Change radar axis names or count
    - Change report type permission matrix
    - Change minimum signal threshold rules
    - Change k-anonymity threshold
    - Change composite score computation
    - Change career map model methodology
    - Change ML model feature sets
    - Change audit record schema
    - Any change affecting minor protection rules

LIFETIME_DATA_COMPATIBILITY:
  CRITICAL_RULE:   Ecoskiller is a lifetime identity system (age 5–60)
                   Users may have data from childhood through adulthood
                   Intelligence dimension changes must account for
                   historical data compatibility — all prior evaluations
                   must remain meaningfully representable in new schema
  version_tagging: All historical reports retain their generation-time
                   model_version and dimension schema reference
  re_computation:  Historical dimension scores NOT retroactively recomputed
                   unless explicitly approved by Governance Board

BACKWARD_COMPATIBILITY_WINDOW: 3 MAJOR versions (longer than other agents
                               due to lifetime data retention requirements)
```

---

## ▶ SECTION 20 — PRIVACY, GDPR & DPDP COMPLIANCE

```yaml
DATA_SUBJECT_RIGHTS:

  RIGHT_TO_ACCESS:
    implementation:     SIR on-demand at any time = right to access own data
    response_time:      Report generated on demand (p95 < 4 seconds)

  RIGHT_TO_PORTABILITY:
    implementation:     PDF export + JSON export of full intelligence profile
    format:             Structured, machine-readable JSON export via API
    scope:              All computed dimension data + audit trail summary

  RIGHT_TO_ERASURE:
    implementation:     User may request data deletion via DATA_PRIVACY_AGENT
    IRGA_behavior:      On deletion request — all IRGA-generated reports marked DELETED
                        Audit records retained (legal obligation) with content redacted
    cascaded_impact:    Feature Store signals marked for deletion → dimensions
                        recomputed without deleted signals on next report generation
    irreversibility:    Deletion is irreversible — user warned before confirmation

  RIGHT_TO_CORRECTION:
    implementation:     User may flag incorrect intelligence signal attribution
    routing:            → GOVERNANCE_AGENT → source agent correction workflow
    IRGA_behavior:      Report regenerated after correction confirmed

  CONSENT_MANAGEMENT:
    granular_consent:   User controls per-dimension, per-audience, per-source consent
    consent_withdrawal: Withdrawing consent for a source → that source excluded
                        from next report generation (not retroactive)
    minor_consent:      For users age < 13: parental consent required for all
                        report types including SIR
    teen_privacy:       Age 13–17: user may set parent visibility controls
                        Platform cannot override teen privacy settings

  DATA_RETENTION:
    intelligence_reports:    Retained for user account lifetime
    audit_records:           7 years minimum (legal compliance)
    recruiter_access_logs:   7 years (talent marketplace audit requirement)
    raw_feature_vectors:     User account lifetime (supports lifetime identity)

GDPR_DPDP_ALIGNMENT:
  data_minimization:    Each report type receives minimum necessary data
  purpose_limitation:   Feature data collected for evaluation, used only for reports
  storage_limitation:   Reports not generated more than needed (scheduling policy)
  privacy_by_design:    IIR k-anonymity, minor protection, consent gates
  accountability:       Audit trail for all data access and report generation
```

---

## ▶ SECTION 21 — ECOSKILLER 300+ MODULE INTEGRATION MAP

| Module | IRGA Integration Role |
|---|---|
| **Intelligence Measurement (Module 2)** | IRGA IS the intelligence report system for this module — powers all 5 report types, radar chart, timeline, prediction, goals |
| **Skill System / Dojo (Module 3)** | Skill intelligence bridges to LIN, LOG, CRE, DEB dimensions; skill gap analysis in SIR and TMIR |
| **Championship System (Module 4)** | Championship records feed LOG, CRE, DEB dimensions; championship intelligence section in all reports |
| **Parent System (Module 5)** | IRGA produces PIR — the core parent dashboard intelligence view |
| **Institute System (Module 6)** | IRGA produces IIR — powers TPO dashboard, cohort analytics, institute effectiveness score |
| **Recruiter System (Module 7)** | IRGA produces RIR — candidate intelligence signal for talent marketplace and AI hiring tools |
| **AI Mentor System (Module 8)** | TMIR intervention recommendations feed AI mentor coaching plans; IRGA growth narrative informs mentor strategy |
| **Integration System (Module 9)** | Tool-sync signals from GitHub, Figma, Notion feed SPA, LOG, CRE dimensions |
| **Creator System (Module 11)** | Creator intelligence profile (CRE + LIN) available in SIR; creator effectiveness fed by ITP signals |
| **Educator System (Module 12)** | TMIR teaching effectiveness signal; educator's own SIR includes teaching ITP score |
| **Social System (Module 14)** | DISTINGUISHED intelligence tier → shareable intelligence card trigger |
| **Trust System (Module 15)** | Parent link verification for PIR; recruiter verification for RIR |
| **Growth Engine (Module 16)** | Intelligence goals, report milestones, achievement triggers all wired through IRGA |
| **Financial System (Module 13)** | Placement probability and career map feed scholarship eligibility signals |

---

## ▶ SECTION 22 — NON-NEGOTIABLE RULES

```
THIS AGENT MUST NOT:

  ✗ Compute raw evaluation scores (all scores come from upstream agents — never IRGA)
  ✗ Allow LLM to compute or modify any intelligence dimension score
  ✗ Generate a report without verified consent record
  ✗ Generate a Recruiter Intelligence Report for any user under age 18 (absolute)
  ✗ Expose individual user data in an Institute Intelligence Report (k-anonymity absolute)
  ✗ Allow cross-tenant data access in any report type
  ✗ Allow a parent to access data the child has explicitly marked private
  ✗ Allow a recruiter to access a user with TALENT_MARKETPLACE_VISIBILITY = DISABLED
  ✗ Allow an educator to access data for students outside their active assignment
  ✗ Make career prescriptions — career mapping is always advisory with explicit labeling
  ✗ Make hiring, certification, or belt recommendations
  ✗ Display a dimension score of 0 when data is insufficient (NULL ≠ 0 — must be distinct)
  ✗ Generate speculative intelligence assessments without evidence-backed signals
  ✗ Produce clinical psychological or medical diagnostic language
  ✗ Modify historical report records (append-only is absolute)
  ✗ Auto-delete audit records under any condition
  ✗ Override GOVERNANCE_AGENT, PRIVACY_AGENT, or COMPLIANCE_AGENT decisions
  ✗ Include identifiable user data in LLM context window
  ✗ Label LLM-generated narrative text as if it were ML-computed data
  ✗ Produce partial reports — all-or-nothing per report type (with defined exceptions)
  ✗ Serve cached reports older than defined TTL per report type
  ✗ Execute outside defined input/output contract scope
  ✗ Allow silent failures — every failure produces a log entry
  ✗ Change dimension weights or signal weight table at runtime
  ✗ Assume missing specifications — halt on ambiguity and escalate
  ✗ Allow any report generation without audit_reference being generated
  ✗ Include financial projections in any report type
  ✗ Bypass lifetime data compatibility requirements on version upgrade
```
